sampling_results.note: All post-filter joins are 100% selective (referential integrity). Only p_name LIKE '%green%' actually filters rows.

join_order.note: 100K entries, 400KB, L1/L2 cache resident across all threads

join_order.structure: bitset[2M] = 250KB for fast O(1) membership test
join_order.note: Bitset over p_partkey range [1,2M]. Also used to drive index lookup.
Filter partsupp by green_parts bitset during build. ~430K entries, ~7MB hash map.
15M entries, ~180MB. Built in parallel before lineitem scan. Probed for year extraction.

join_order.probe: lineitem via lineitem_partkey_hash index for ~107,500 green part keys
join_order.note: Index-driven: for each green partkey, probe index to get row positions, then access l_suppkey/l_orderkey/l_quantity/l_extendedprice/l_discount. No full scan.

logical_plan.aggregation_note: GROUP BY (nation, o_year): 25 nations × 7 years = 175 max groups. Use double agg[25][8] indexed by (nation_code, year-1992). Thread-local, merge after parallel phase.

filter_pushdown.method: strstr substring scan on fixed-width char[56]
bitset lookup during sequential partsupp scan

year_extraction.note: Build int32_t year_lut[?] covering TPC-H date range 1992-1998 (7 entries). O(1) per row. See date-operations.md formula.

data_structures.green_parts_filter: bitset over [1,2M] = 250KB, fits in L2 cache per thread
data_structures.partsupp_map: compact_hash_map(pre_sized 860007) keyed int64 composite (pkey<<20|skey) -> double supplycost, ~7MB
data_structures.orders_map: compact_hash_map(pre_sized 30000007) keyed int32 orderkey -> int32 orderdate, ~180MB
data_structures.aggregation: direct_array double[25][8] thread-local, indexed by (nationkey, o_year-1992)

build_phase_order: 2. supplier -> direct array (100K rows, parallel scan)
3. part -> green_parts bitset (2M rows, parallel scan with strstr)
4. partsupp -> composite hash map (8M rows, parallel scan, filter by bitset)
5. orders -> hash map (15M rows, parallel build)

build_parallelism.part_scan: parallel 64 threads, 31250 rows/thread, local green-part lists, merge into shared bitset via atomic or private sub-ranges
build_parallelism.partsupp_scan: parallel 64 threads, 125000 rows/thread, thread-local maps merged into global
build_parallelism.orders_build: parallel 64 threads, 234375 rows/thread, partition-based hash map build or concurrent insert

probe_parallelism.note: Each thread owns a disjoint subset of green_parts. Independently probes index, partsupp, supplier, orders maps. Zero synchronization during probe — all build structures are read-only.

parallelism.synchronization: lock_free — build structures are read-only during probe; agg arrays are thread-local

index_usage.purpose: index_driven_access: retrieve ~3.2M lineitem row positions for ~107K green partkeys, skipping 56.8M lineitem rows entirely

scan_details.part: sequential_full_scan 2M rows × 56 bytes = 112MB (strstr on each row)
scan_details.partsupp: sequential_full_scan 8M rows × 12 bytes = 96MB (ps_partkey+ps_suppkey+ps_supplycost), filtered by bitset
scan_details.lineitem: index_driven: mmap index (50MB) + positions (~12MB) + 6 column arrays random-access for 3.2M rows (~150MB effective)

phase_breakdown.orders_hash_build: ~25 ms (120 MB scan + 15M hash inserts, parallel)
phase_breakdown.lineitem_index_probe: ~30 ms (index lookup + ~150 MB scattered col reads + hash probes × 3.2M rows, parallel 64 threads)

notes: Index eliminates 94.6% of lineitem rows (57M of 60M), reducing dominant I/O from 3.7GB to ~370MB total.
All joins post-filter are 100% selective (empirically confirmed via sampling) — no further cardinality reduction.
64-thread parallelism applied to all phases. Build structures are constructed in parallel then used read-only during parallel probe.
Aggregation output is 175 groups max (25 nations × 7 years) — direct 2D array, zero hash overhead.
HDD: mmap + kernel readahead partially mitigates random access cost in lineitem column arrays for 3.2M rows.
